summary:
The Confirmation Of Congressional Observer Access Act of 2024 (COCOA Act of 2024) amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to require states to grant access to designated congressional election observers during federal elections.

The law adds a new Section 304 to Title III of the Help America Vote Act. It begins by stating Congress's position that, under Article I of the Constitution, it already has the authority to send observers to polling locations and any location where votes are processed, scanned, tabulated, canvassed, recounted, audited, or certified. The bill states that it does not establish new authorities but provides a statutory reference for existing congressional activity.

Under the new provision, states must give each designated congressional election observer full access to observe all elements of election administration, including areas where ballots are cast, processed, tabulated, recounted, audited, or certified, including pre- and post-election procedures. Observers may ask questions of election officials and workers.

The bill restricts observer conduct: observers may not handle ballots or election equipment, advocate for any candidate or position, reduce ballot secrecy or voter privacy, or interfere with voters or election administrators.

State or local election officials may remove an observer if they have a reasonable basis to believe the observer has engaged in or will imminently engage in intimidation, deceptive practices prohibited by federal law, or disruption of voting or ballot processing. The mere presence of an observer is not sufficient grounds for removal. If an observer is removed, the official must, within 24 hours, notify the chair and ranking minority member of the House Committee on House Administration or the Senate Committee on Rules and Administration and provide written reasons. The relevant committee leaders may send a replacement observer.

A "designated congressional election observer" is a House or Senate employee designated in writing by the chair or ranking minority member of the relevant committee. "State" includes the 50 states, the District of Columbia, and listed U.S. territories.

The Act also makes conforming enforcement and clerical amendments. It was approved October 4, 2024.
